title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken Recipe - Crispy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken! Get our easy, authentic recipe for crispy, sweet, and spicy fried chicken with a delicious sauce.

seo.imageAlt: Plate of crispy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken coated in sweet and spicy sauce, garnished with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and savory with our homemade Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken!

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring perfectly crispy double-fried chicken coated in a luscious, sticky sauce that balances sweet, spicy, and savory flavors. This iconic Korean street food offers an irresistible crunch followed by a burst of complex tastes, making it a truly addictive culinary experience. Garnished with sesame seeds and sometimes peanuts, it is a delightful treat perfect for any occasion.

instructions.title: Prepare and Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and pat dry the chicken pieces with paper towels. In a large bowl, season the chicken with salt, black pepper, ginger powder, garlic powder, and soju or rice wine. Mix well to ensure all pieces are coated. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Prepare the Frying Batter
instructions.text: In a separate large bowl, whisk together the potato starch or cornstarch, all-purpose flour, and baking powder. In a small bowl, lightly beat the egg and add it to the dry ingredients along with the cold water. Mix until a smooth, thick batter forms. Do not overmix; a few lumps are fine. The consistency should be like pancake batter, thick enough to cling to the chicken.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large, deep pot or Dutch oven to 325°F (160°C). Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated, and carefully place it into the hot oil. Do not overcrowd the pot; fry in batches. Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until the chicken is lightly golden and cooked through. Remove the chicken and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain excess oil. Let it rest for 5-10 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken rests, combine all Yangnyeom sauce ingredients: gochujang, soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, rice vinegar, honey or corn syrup, brown sugar, ketchup, and sesame oil in a medium saucepan. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Keep warm on very low heat.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Perfection
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 350°F (175°C). Carefully return the partially fried chicken pieces to the hot oil for a second fry. This step is crucial for achieving the signature extra-crispy texture. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until the chicken is deep golden brown and exceptionally crispy. Remove from oil and place on a clean wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken with Sauce
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the hot, double-fried chicken into the warm Yangnyeom sauce. Toss gently but quickly to ensure every piece is evenly coated with the delicious sticky sauce. Work in batches if necessary to avoid cooling down the chicken too much.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Arrange the coated Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken on a serving platter. Garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped peanuts, if desired. Serve immediately while hot and crispy for the best experience. It pairs wonderfully with pickled radish or a simple side salad.

tips: For the crispiest chicken, double-frying is non-negotiable. The first fry cooks the chicken, and the second fry at a higher temperature creates that irresistible crunch.
Adjust the amount of gochujang and brown sugar in the sauce to suit your preferred level of spiciness and sweetness. For a milder version, reduce gochujang and add more ketchup.
Yangnyeom chicken is best served immediately to enjoy its maximum crispiness. If you have leftovers, reheat them in an air fryer or oven to regain some crunch, though the sauce will be less sticky.
article:
Get ready to embark on a culinary journey that has taken the world by storm: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. More than just a dish, it's a global phenomenon, celebrated for its irresistible crunch, juicy interior, and that glorious, sticky, sweet-and-spicy sauce. Forget everything you thought you knew about fried chicken; Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken offers a unique textural and flavor experience that sets it apart, making it a beloved staple in Korean cuisine and a highly sought-after treat worldwide. If you’ve ever craved chicken that’s impossibly crispy and coated in a sauce that hits every note – sweet, savory, spicy, and tangy – then you’re in the right place. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know to recreate this legendary dish right in your own kitchen, bringing the vibrant flavors of Korea to your dinner table.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, often simply referred to as "Yangnyeom Chicken," translates literally to "seasoned sauce fried chicken." What truly defines this dish is its signature sauce, a thick, glossy, and intensely flavorful coating that envelops each piece of perfectly fried chicken. Unlike traditional Western fried chicken, which is often served plain or with a simple gravy, Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is all about the sauce. This iconic sauce typically features a base of gochujang (Korean chili paste) and gochugaru (Korean chili powder), balanced with sweet elements like sugar, corn syrup, or honey, and savory notes from soy sauce, garlic, ginger, and a touch of tang from rice vinegar.

Beyond the sauce, the chicken itself boasts an unparalleled crispiness, achieved through a unique double-frying method. This technique ensures an incredibly light, airy, and shatteringly crispy crust that holds up remarkably well, even when coated in the rich sauce. The result is a harmonious blend of textures and tastes: tender, succulent chicken beneath a super-crispy exterior, all brought together by that unforgettable, complex Yangnyeom sauce. It's an addictive combination that makes it a cornerstone of Korean chicken recipes.

## Ingredients You Need

To make authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, you'll need a few key ingredients, some of which are specific to Korean cooking but are readily available in Asian markets or online.

**For the Chicken & Frying:**
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 lbs chicken wings, drumsticks, or boneless thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces. Wings are traditional and popular.
*   **Marinade (Optional but Recommended):** 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 tsp minced garlic, 1/2 tsp black pepper.
*   **Dredging/Batter:** 1 cup potato starch (preferred for crispiness) or cornstarch, 1/2 cup all-purpose flour, 1 tsp baking powder, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper, 1 cup cold water or soda water (for extra crispiness).
*   **Frying Oil:** 4-6 cups neutral oil with a high smoke point, such as vegetable, canola, peanut, or grapeseed oil.

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   **Gochujang:** 3-4 tbsp Korean chili paste (adjust to your spice preference).
*   **Gochugaru:** 1-2 tsp Korean chili powder (optional, for extra heat and color).
*   **Soy Sauce:** 2 tbsp light soy sauce.
*   **Sugar/Sweetener:** 2-3 tbsp sugar, corn syrup, or honey (corn syrup is traditional for glossiness).
*   **Rice Vinegar:** 1 tbsp.
*   **Garlic:** 2-3 cloves, minced.
*   **Ginger:** 1/2 tsp grated (optional).
*   **Sesame Oil:** 1 tsp.
*   **Water:** 2-3 tbsp, to thin the sauce if needed.

**For Garnish:**
*   Toasted sesame seeds
*   Chopped roasted peanuts
*   Thinly sliced green onions/scallions

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making delicious Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for crispy, saucy perfection.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Thoroughly wash and pat the chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. This is crucial for crispiness. If marinating, combine the chicken with soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and black pepper. Let it sit for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator, or up to 2 hours. Pat dry again before proceeding.
2.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the potato starch (or cornstarch), all-purpose flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Gradually add the cold water or soda water, whisking until you have a smooth, thin batter, similar to pancake batter. It should be thin enough to coat the chicken lightly.
3.  **First Fry:** Heat the frying oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven to 325-350°F (160-175°C). Dip each chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it’s evenly coated, letting any excess drip off. Carefully place the chicken into the hot oil, working in batches to avoid overcrowding the pot, which lowers the oil temperature. Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly golden. Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon or spider and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Let it rest for 5-10 minutes.
4.  **Second Fry:** Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil, again in batches if necessary. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and incredibly crispy. This double-frying is key to achieving that signature crunch. Remove the chicken and place it back on the wire rack to drain any excess oil.
5.  **Make the Yangnyeom Sauce:** While the chicken is resting or during the second fry, prepare the sauce. In a medium saucepan, combine gochujang, gochugaru (if using), soy sauce, sugar/sweetener, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger (if using), sesame oil, and water. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens slightly and becomes glossy (about 3-5 minutes). Taste and adjust sweetness or spice as desired.
6.  **Coat the Chicken:** Immediately after the second fry, while the chicken is still hot and crispy, transfer it to a large mixing bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss quickly and thoroughly until every piece is evenly coated.
7.  **Garnish and Serve:** Transfer the coated Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken to a serving platter. Garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds, chopped peanuts, and sliced green onions. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving the perfect Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken involves a few expert tricks:

*   **Pat Dry, Pat Dry, Pat Dry:** Any moisture on the chicken will lead to a soggy crust and dangerous oil splatters. Be diligent with paper towels!
*   **The Power of Potato Starch:** While cornstarch works, potato starch creates an even lighter and crispier crust that holds up better to the sauce.
*   **Don't Skimp on Double Frying:** This isn't an optional step. The first fry cooks the chicken, and the rest period allows internal moisture to redistribute. The second, hotter fry creates an incredibly crisp exterior that locks in the juices.
*   **Maintain Oil Temperature:** Use a thermometer and fry in batches. Overcrowding the pot drops the oil temperature, resulting in greasy, less crispy chicken.
*   **Balance the Sauce:** Taste your Yangnyeom sauce as it simmers. Adjust the gochujang for spice, sugar for sweetness, and vinegar for tang until it suits your palate.
*   **Coat While Hot:** Toss the chicken in the sauce immediately after its second fry. The heat helps the sauce adhere better and prevents the chicken from cooling down too much.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Korean fried chicken is best enjoyed fresh from the pan. While it can be reheated, the crispiness will diminish over time.

## Serving Suggestions

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a star on its own, but it pairs wonderfully with a few traditional Korean accompaniments:

*   **Pickled Radish (Mu-saengchae):** The tangy, refreshing crunch of pickled radish is the perfect counterpoint to the rich, spicy chicken.
*   **Steamed White Rice:** A simple bowl of rice helps soak up any extra sauce and balances the intense flavors.
*   **Coleslaw or Green Salad:** A fresh, crisp salad offers a nice contrast in texture and flavor.
*   **Korean Beer (Maekju):** The "chimaek" (chicken + maekju) culture is famous in Korea for a reason! A cold lager is the ultimate pairing.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a masterpiece, you can experiment to suit your preferences:

*   **Adjusting Spice Levels:** For less heat, reduce the amount of gochujang and gochugaru. For more spice, add extra gochugaru or a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Sweetness Factor:** Increase or decrease the sugar/corn syrup in the sauce to match your desired level of sweetness. Honey can also be used for a slightly different flavor profile.
*   **Gluten-Free Option:** Use tamari instead of soy sauce and ensure your potato starch is pure, avoiding any flour in the batter.
*   **Vegetarian Alternative:** You can apply the same double-frying and saucing technique to firm tofu, cauliflower florets, or even king oyster mushrooms for a delicious vegetarian "fried chicken."
*   **Other Korean Chicken Recipes:** Explore other popular Korean fried chicken sauces like Ganjang (Soy Garlic) chicken or Honey Butter chicken for a different flavor experience, using the same crispy double-fried chicken as your base.

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is more than just a meal; it's an experience. The satisfying crunch, the tender meat, and that incredible, complex sauce create a symphony of flavors and textures that will leave you craving more. With these detailed instructions and tips, you're well-equipped to master this beloved Korean dish and impress your friends and family with your culinary skills. So, gather your ingredients, fire up the fryer, and get ready to enjoy the best Korean fried chicken you’ve ever tasted!
